Quality Assurance Analyst
anishkumarsankar2023@gmail.com | +91 93633 84114
Chennai TN
PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY
 4 years of industry experience in the area of Software Testing (Manual, Web,
Mobile and API) with a solid understanding of Test Planning, Test Design, Test
Execution and Defect Reporting & Tracking
 Expertise in Understanding and Analyzing Test Requirements, Tracking changes
and maintenance of Test Requirements
 Strong Experience in Automating Web Application Testing using Selenium
WebDriver with Cucumber framework
 Strong Experience in Java Programming, Junit and TestNG
 Well versed with Handling Elements in Selenium WebDriver
 Strong Experience writing Test cases using Element locators, WebDriver methods
and Java programming features
 Strong Experience in handling tools like GITHUB
 Executing Selenium Test Cases and Reporting defects
 Experience in developing test cases based on requirements in JIRA
 Experience in POSTMAN Tool and Rest Assured for API Integration
 Good experience using Appium for iOS and Android applications.
 Experience in executing queries using MYSQL
 Good written, communication, interpersonal skills, proven team player with an
analytical mind bent to problem solving and delivering solutions
WORK HISTORY
Quality Assurance Analyst | Gembrill Technologies | Chennai |
01/2022 - 10/2025
Client: Koncert Sales Engagement Platform
Domain: Customer Relationship Management
Description: Koncert is a Sales Engagement Platform which acts as a standard
interface between the clients and all the sales representatives and is used to increase
sales performance through Calls, Emails and other Social platforms.
Client: Koncert Cadence
Domain: Telecom and CRM
Description: Cadence is an add on product of Koncert which is used for continuous
follow-up and tracking tool to generate business call, email, text, social media and
other touches.
Responsibilities:
 Scripting using Java programming.
 Good at finding X-path locators.
 Implemented automation using Selenium WebDriver and Maven.

-- 1 of 2 --

 Developed and executed the automation test scripts using the BDD framework.
 Developing BDD tests using Cucumber by writing feature files and step
definitions.
 Execute test cases manually to identify bugs, and ensure the application meets
functional and business requirements.
 Developing reusable functional components in the framework.
 Maintained locators using the Page Object Model.
 Updated framework functions when needed.
 Involved in pushing, pulling, and merging the source code from GitHub.
 Reviewing the test reports and preparing the test summary report.
 Preparation of test scenarios, test cases, test execution and test results.
 Used Postman API to get convenient, visual display of query result Tested
changes to user interface, application functionality and database access as
required by the organizations
 Skilled in Appium for Mobile Automation Testing, including configuring the
Appium test environment, creating MobileDriver classes with desired capabilities,
and identifying mobile elements using Appium Inspector
 Reporting any defects or Observation on day-to-day basis
 Attending Agile meetings on assigned projects
 Understanding the Business Requirement Specification.
 Designing Test Cases, Execution of the test cases and reporting the bugs.
 Conducted Functional testing with Valid and Invalid inputs for positive and
negative testing.
 Executing Tests and analyzing results as per client requirements.
 Detecting the bugs and classifying them based on the severity and reporting.
 Being part of all requirement gatherings and decisions to understand and
deliver thoughts.
 Creating Test scenarios and Collecting Test Data.
 Tracking Defects and Select Test cases for Regression Testing.

Bachelor of Engineering

that focuses on automating and managing test executions within financial systems. The application is
designed to simplify the creation, configuration, and monitoring of test cases, test jobs, and test

PRODUCTION ISSUES HANDLED & RESOLUTIONS
Issue: Application crash due to unhandled runtime exceptions in Angular components
Fix: Implemented global error handling using ErrorHandler class and added proper try-catch
blocks in service calls to gracefully handle API failures.
Issue: Broken layout/UI inconsistencies in production despite working in development
Fix: Identified mismatched CSS loading due to AOT (Ahead-of-Time) compilation differences
and used ng build --prod locally for testing before deployment.
Issue: Angular routes not loading properly when directly accessed via URL (404 error)
Fix: Configured server-side fallback using index.html for SPA support in nginx or Apache.
Issue: API failures due to CORS errors after moving to production
Fix: Worked with backend team to configure proper CORS headers and tested endpoints
through Postman and Angular HTTP calls.
Issue: Large bundle size causing slow initial load
Fix: Used Angular Lazy Loading, optimized images, removed unused modules, and enabled
production build optimizations (--prod, --build-optimizer, --aot).
Issue: Unresponsive Angular application after deployment due to environment
misconfiguration
Fix: Ensured correct environment.prod.ts variables were used in deployment and
environment-specific build commands (ng build --configuration=production) were followed.
Issue: Angular CLI commands not working due to PowerShell script restrictions
Fix: Modified execution policy using Set-ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned with admin rights and
ensured Angular CLI path is added to system environment variables.
Issue: API performance degradation due to inefficient SQL queries
Fix: Used @Query with proper indexing and query tuning; analyzed slow queries using
EXPLAIN plans and optimized them.
Issue: Memory leaks or high memory usage under load
Fix: Performed heap dump analysis, optimized caching with @Cacheable, and removed
unnecessary object creation in services.
Issue: Unstable deployments due to missing environment variables or wrong configurations
Fix: Used @ConfigurationProperties with profile-based .yml files, and validated required
properties using @Validated.
Issue: Long application startup time in production
Fix: Delayed non-critical bean initialization, optimized the number of loaded dependencies,
and switched to lighter logging configuration for production.
